data.message: productive cough with fever and shortness of breath

result.explanation: The symptom pattern of fever, cough, productive_cough associated with cough is consistent with the ranked differential diagnoses based on clinical case memory and probabilistic inference.
reasoning_path: L2 Similarity: 5 cases found, top Dx: pneumonia
L3 Bayes: top Dx pneumonia (33%), entropy=1.16

pathway.title: Acute Viral URTI / Cough Pathway
pathway.description: Cough <3 weeks without red flags — likely viral, supportive care.

steps.action: Honey 1–2 tsp PRN (adults), warm beverages, humidifier
Guaifenesin 400mg q4h PRN (expectorant for productive cough)
Dextromethorphan 15–30mg q6-8h PRN (dry cough)
Ibuprofen or Acetaminophen PRN for fever/myalgias
Hand hygiene, mask use around vulnerable individuals, rest
Return if cough >3 weeks, hemoptysis, fever >5 days, or SOB
steps.rationale: Rule out pneumonia, pertussis, or sinusitis

contraindications: Dextromethorphan: MAOI use, certain SSRIs (serotonin syndrome risk)

medicationsPrescribed: Guaifenesin 400mg q4h PRN (expectorant for productive cough)
Ibuprofen or Acetaminophen PRN for fever/myalgias
followupScheduled: Return if cough >3 weeks, hemoptysis, fever >5 days, or SOB
contraindications: Dextromethorphan: MAOI use, certain SSRIs (serotonin syndrome risk)

data.note:
VISIT NOTE — March 13, 2026
Patient: 45yo male
Chief Complaint: cough

Subjective:
productive cough with fever and shortness of breath

Differential Diagnosis:
  1. pneumonia (37%)
  2. viral URI (10%)
  3. COPD exacerbation (7%)
  4. tuberculosis (7%)
  5. meningococcemia (3%)

Disposition: URGENT CARE

Clinical Reasoning:
The symptom pattern of fever, cough, productive_cough associated with cough is consistent with the ranked differential diagnoses based on clinical case memory and probabilistic inference.

data.text:
DISCHARGE INSTRUCTIONS

Please visit an Urgent Care clinic within the next 2-4 hours.

RETURN PRECAUTIONS — Return to care immediately if you develop:
• Chest pain or severe difficulty breathing
• High fever (>39°C / 102°F)
• Confusion, severe headache, or stiff neck
• Severe worsening of any symptom

This assessment is for triage guidance only and does not replace in-person medical evaluation.
